steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
Since angle B is the right angle, AC is the hypotenuse. We have:
$$AC^2 = (16\sqrt{29})^2 + (30\sqrt{29})^2$$
Squaring each term:
$$AC^2 = 256 \cdot 29 + 900 \cdot 29 = 29(256 + 900) = 29 \cdot 1156$$

steps.title: Simplify the radical
steps.content:
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$AC = \sqrt{29 \cdot 1156} = \sqrt{29} \cdot \sqrt{1156} = \sqrt{29} \cdot 34 = 34\sqrt{29}$$
B is correct.
